questions.question: Has India experienced a long-term change in meteorological and hydrological droughts since the late 19th century, and which regions show intensified drought?
questions.supporting_quote: There was no observed overall long-term change of both meteorological droughts and hydrological droughts over India during 1870–2018 (Mishra, 2020), but there were strong trends towards drying of soil moisture in north-central India (Ganeshi et al., 2020) and intensified droughts in north-west India

questions.question: Has human-induced climate change contributed to increases in agricultural and ecological droughts?
questions.supporting_quote: Human-induced climate change has contributed to increases in agricultural and ecological droughts

questions.question: How does the rate of global mean sea level rise since 1900 compare with previous centuries?
questions.supporting_quote: Global mean sea level has risen faster since 1900 than over any preceding century in at least the last 3000 years

questions.question: How much is relative sea level projected to rise in the oceans around Asia by 2081-2100 under low versus high emissions scenarios?
questions.supporting_quote: Relative sea level rise is very likely to continue in the oceans around Asia. Regional mean RSLR projections for the oceans around Asia range from 0.3–0.5 m under SSP1-2.6 to 0.7–0.8 m under SSP5-8.5

questions.question: Has the number of intense tropical cyclones in the Bay of Bengal increased since the mid-1980s?
questions.supporting_quote: There was an increase in the number and intensification rate of intense tropical cyclones (TC), such as Category 4–5 (wind speeds >58 m s –1), in the Western North Pacific (WNP) and Bay of Bengal since the mid-1980s

questions.question: How has the frequency of marine heatwaves changed since the 1980s, and what role did human influence play?
questions.supporting_quote: Marine heatwaves have approximately doubled in frequency since the 1980s ( high confidence ), and human influence has very likely contributed to most of them since at least 2006

questions.question: In how many regions, and on which continents, are agricultural and ecological droughts projected to increase relative to 1850-1900?
questions.supporting_quote: agricultural and ecological droughts are projected in a few regions in all inhabited continents except Asia

questions.question: How much more often are extreme sea level events that historically occurred once per century projected to occur due to relative sea level rise?
questions.supporting_quote: extreme sea level events that occurred once per century in the recent past are projected to occur at least annually at more than half of all tide gauge locations

questions.question: By how much is the proportion of the most intense (Category 4-5) tropical cyclones projected to change at the global scale?
questions.supporting_quote: The proportion of intense tropical cyclones (Category 4–5) and peak wind speeds of the most intense tropical cyclones are projected to increase at the global scale

questions.question: On which continents has heavy precipitation likely increased at the continental scale?
questions.supporting_quote: Heavy precipitation has likely increased on the continental scale over three continents: North America, Europe, and Asia

questions.question: How have the frequency and intensity of heavy precipitation events changed at the global scale and across land regions?
questions.supporting_quote: The frequency and intensity of heavy precipitation events have increased at the global scale and over a majority of land regions

questions.question: How is the frequency of rare heavy precipitation events (such as 10-year and 50-year events) projected to change at 4 degrees of global warming?
questions.supporting_quote: The increase in the frequency of heavy precipitation events will be non-linear with more warming and will be higher for rarer events ( high confidence), with a likely doubling and tripling in the frequency of 10-year and 50-year events, respectively, compared to the recent past at 4°C of global warming

questions.question: What factors cause projected increases in extreme precipitation intensity to vary between regions?
questions.supporting_quote: Increases in the intensity of extreme precipitation at regional scales will vary, depending on the amount of regional warming, changes in atmospheric circulation and storm dynamics

questions.question: What is the main driver of observed changes in hot and cold extremes, and with what confidence globally and by continent?
questions.supporting_quote: Human-induced greenhouse gas forcing is the main driver of the observed changes in hot and cold extremes on the global scale ( virtually certain) and on most continents

questions.question: Could some recent hot extreme events have occurred without human influence on the climate system?
questions.supporting_quote: Some recent hot extreme events would have been extremely unlikely to occur without human influence on the climate system

questions.question: Has fire weather - compound hot, dry and windy conditions - become more frequent in some regions?
questions.supporting_quote: Medium confidence that fire weather, i.e. compound hot, dry and windy events, have become more frequent in some regions

questions.question: As global warming increases, what happens to the occurrence of extreme events that are unprecedented in the observed record?
questions.supporting_quote: The occurrence of extreme events unprecedented in the observed record will rise with increasing global warming

questions.question: Have changes in anthropogenic aerosol concentrations affected trends in hot extremes in some regions?
questions.supporting_quote: Changes in anthropogenic aerosol concentrations have likely affected trends in hot extremes in some regions

questions.question: Have irrigation and crop expansion influenced summer hot extremes in some regions such as the Midwestern USA?
questions.supporting_quote: Irrigation and crop expansion have attenuated increases in summer hot extremes in some regions, such as the Midwestern USA

questions.question: How will the number of hot days and hot nights and the frequency of heatwaves change over most land areas with warming?
questions.supporting_quote: The number of hot days and hot nights and the length, frequency, and/or intensity of warm spells or heatwaves will increase over most land areas

questions.question: In which regions is the temperature of the hottest days projected to increase fastest relative to global warming?
questions.supporting_quote: The highest increase of temperature of hottest days is projected in some mid-latitude and semi-arid regions and in the South American Monsoon region

questions.question: Where is the temperature of the coldest days projected to increase fastest, and by how much relative to global warming?
questions.supporting_quote: The highest increase of temperature of coldest days is projected in Arctic regions, at about three times the rate of global warming

questions.question: With what confidence is the increase in frequency and intensity of heavy precipitation assessed across continents and AR6 regions?
questions.supporting_quote: The increase in frequency and intensity is extremely likely for most continents and very likely for most AR6 regions

questions.question: At the global scale, what determines the rate at which heavy precipitation intensifies as the climate warms?
questions.supporting_quote: the intensification of heavy precipitation will follow the rate of increase in the maximum amount of moisture that the atmosphere can hold as it warms

questions.question: Can climate models capture the large-scale spatial distribution of precipitation extremes over land?
questions.supporting_quote: Models are able to capture the large-scale spatial distribution of precipitation extremes over land
